rulings.Case Title: RELIABLE TREE EXPERTS VS. THE DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Date For Reliable Tree Experts' Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 18, 2010, line 9, PETITIONER RELIABLE TREE EXPERTS Notice of Hearing Date for Reliable Tree Experts' Writ of Adminstrative Mandamus. Writ is denied. The Court employs independent judgment/de novo review and accords little to no deference to Respondent's decision. Petitioner was hired to prune and remove trees, and approximately 80% of the work involved removal. Removing and pruning trees is "usual" work with regard to roadways, and petitioner's work meets the definition of maintenence under 8 CCR 16000. Courts will liberally construe prevailing wage statutes. (See State Building & Construction Trades Council of California vs. Duncan (2008) 162 Cal.App. 4th 289, 306.) The San Bernardino decision is not binding, that tree removal was performed on privately owned residential land, and was a one-time project designed to remove dead, dying, or diseased trees. Here, the project involves a normal and recurring activity of maintaining the public highway. =(302/CWW)
